Title: SUBSTANDARD STARCH GRAIN7 regulates starch grain size and endosperm development in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific DUF1001 domain-containing integral membrane protein; AtCRL homolog
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate and determinant of grain quality and industrial starch-use properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal compound starch grain formation, starch accumulation, and proper endosperm development; loss of function causes enlarged spherical starch grains, reduced starch/protein/lipid accumulation, floury endosperm, and lower grain weight
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSG7 was cloned as LOC_Os11g32160, and complementation restored while CRISPR knockout phenocopied the mutant. 2. SSG7 encodes an amyloplast membrane DUF1001 protein required for normal starch grain size and compound starch grain formation. 3. ssg7 mutants showed enlarged starch grains, reduced total starch and amylose, altered amylopectin chain-length distribution, and defective endosperm development. 4. SSG7 forms homodimers and associates with SSG4, SSG6, and translocon components, linking protein import machinery to starch grain development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation by overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; immunoblotting; subcellular localization with GFP fusion and immunogold EM; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation imaging; co-immunoprecipitation; IP-MS; metabolite/starch phenotyping
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SSG7; SSG4; SSG6; TOC159; TOC120; TOC90; TOC75; TOC64; TOC34; TIC110
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed in all examined tissues; highest protein accumulation in leaf and stem; abundant through late endosperm development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broadly present in rice tissues; abundant in developing endosperm until late stages
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal field growth conditions; developing grains analyzed mainly at 3, 6, 9, and 18 DAF
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize UBIQUITIN promoter (constitutive); native SSG7 promoter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for biotechnological modification of starch grain size and starch quality in rice; useful for breeding programs aiming to optimize grain starch properties, potentially together with SSG6 and SSG4-related modules
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces starch, protein, and lipid accumulation, decreases grain weight and seed setting rate, and causes floury endosperm; complete knockout may impair male gamete transmission
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SSG7 is functionally divergent from Arabidopsis CRL and cyanobacterial CpcT, as their overexpression did not rescue ssg7. Enlargement of starch grains is attributed to increased starch granule initiation events within a single amyloplast rather than larger granule size. No apparent gene dosage effect in reciprocal F1 endosperms.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS/LC-MS/MS of GFP-SSG7 immunoprecipitates; no public accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TIC236/DEK5 homolog with C-terminal DUF490 domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/amyloplast inner envelope membrane (inferred from TIC236 homology and cited prior work)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate and determinant of grain quality and industrial starch-use properties
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Compound starch grain development and endosperm development via plastid protein import/translocon association
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein complex-mediated control associated with translocon linkage
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SSG7-associated SG development module; TOC75-linked translocon function
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with SSG7 to support translocon-associated functions required for normal starch grain development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator of starch grain development; acts synergistically with SSG7 to control starch grain size
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports normal starch grain size and endosperm development; genetic interaction with ssg7 indicates synergistic control of starch grain development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSG4 was identified in GFP-SSG7 immunoprecipitates by IP-MS. 2. The DUF490 domain of SSG4 directly interacted with the DUF1001 domain of SSG7 in pull-down assays. 3. Genetic analyses showed SSG4 and SSG7 act synergistically in regulating starch grain development, with double-mutant recovery hindered by male gametophyte defects.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; in vitro GST pull-down; genetic crossing and segregation analyses
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SSG7; TOC75 (from prior homologous evidence cited)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional complex/module with SSG7 and SSG6
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal field growth conditions during grain development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target in engineering starch grain morphology through translocon-associated SG development complexes, though full-length cloning difficulty may limit immediate deployment
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined mutant condition with ssg7 shows impaired male gamete transmission; defects likely compromise grain quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified here as regulatory because it functions as a non-enzymatic translocon-associated regulator of SG development rather than a catalytic starch biosynthetic enzyme. Full-length SSG4 could not be cloned in this study due to bacterial toxicity.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS/LC-MS/MS of GFP-SSG7 immunoprecipitates; no public accession reported

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Amyloplast envelope regulator; translocon-associated SG development factor
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ACS10/ACS12-like aminotransferase homolog; noncanonical ACS-like protein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate and determinant of grain quality and industrial starch-use properties
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Starch grain size control and endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein complex-mediated modulation of SG development; partial functional redundancy with SSG7
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports normal starch grain development through association with SSG7-containing module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important regulator essential for normal starch grain development; can compensate for SSG7 loss when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal starch grain size and endosperm development; overexpression rescues ssg7 defective endosperm and enlarged starch grains
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SSG6 was identified in GFP-SSG7 immunoprecipitates and its interaction with SSG7 was validated by immunoblotting and luciferase complementation imaging. 2. SSG6 overexpression in the ssg7 background restored translucent endosperm and normal-sized starch grains. 3. SSG6 and SSG7 therefore show partial functional redundancy in controlling starch grain size and endosperm development.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; immunoblot validation of co-precipitation; luciferase complementation imaging; transgenic overexpression rescue
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in a protein module/complex with SSG7 and SSG4
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal field growth conditions during grain development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to compensate SSG7-related defects and tune starch grain morphology; candidate for gene stacking with SSG7-centered SG development machinery
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Biological role remains incompletely resolved; perturbation of this envelope-associated module may affect endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although homologous to ACS10/ACS12-like aminotransferases, the study argues SSG7-related SG phenotypes are unlikely to be explained simply by amino acid metabolism changes. Classified as regulatory because the paper studies its non-enzymatic role in SG development complex behavior.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS/LC-MS/MS of GFP-SSG7 immunoprecipitates; no public accession reported
